teaching_card.title: Proportions and Rates (Easy)
teaching_card.body: A proportion sets two ratios equal. Cross-multiply to solve for an unknown. Rates compare two quantities with different units, like miles per hour; scale them up or down proportionally.
key_points: Keep units consistent on each side.

screens.title: Set up the ratio
screens.body: A proportion sets two equal ratios: a/b = x/c. Keep the same units in the same positions (miles over hours on both sides), then cross-multiply and solve for the unknown.
key_points: Match units across the equal ratios.

screens.title: Unit rate first
screens.body: Often it's quickest to find the unit rate (the amount for ONE) by dividing, then multiply by however many you need. '5 cost $15' -> $3 each -> 8 cost $24.
key_points: Then multiply the unit rate by the new quantity.

screens.title: Direct vs. inverse
screens.body: Direct: more of one means more of the other (more hours, more miles). Inverse: more of one means LESS of the other (more workers, less time). For inverse, the product stays constant: w₁·h₁ = w₂·h₂.
key_points: Direct -> same direction; ratio is constant.
Inverse -> opposite direction; the product is constant.

screens.title: Try the moves
screens.body: 3 machines in 2 hours -> 12 in x hours? Direct: 3/2 = 12/x -> x = 8. But 4 workers take 6 hours, so 3 workers (inverse) take 4·6 ÷ 3 = 8 hours — fewer workers, more time.
